message:
fix(engine): insert Pop ops after consuming statements in IR1 lowering and clear registers on call

lowering_pipeline.rs (+14 lines):
Add Ir1Op::Pop after every statement and expression that leaves a value
on the conceptual stack but whose result is discarded. Without these
Pop instructions, the register-based interpreter accumulates stale
values that leak across statements, causing incorrect results in
sequences like:
  x = foo();  // leaves return value on stack
  y = bar();  // stack depth is off by one

Affected sites: ExpressionStatement, VariableDeclaration initializers,
ExportDefault, ExportNamedClause, IfStatement condition, ForStatement
condition, WhileStatement condition, DoWhileStatement condition,
SwitchStatement discriminant comparison, TryCatch binding,
FunctionDeclaration, and conditional (ternary) expressions.

baseline_interpreter.rs (+8 lines):
Clear all registers in a new call frame to Value::Undefined before
copying arguments. Previously, registers beyond the argument count
retained values from the caller's frame, which could cause data
leakage between calls with different argument counts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
